chapter_title.en: Chapter on What is Permissible and Impermissible for the Muhrim to Do and Use of All Types

text: : «اَلْمُحْرِمُ يُلْقِي عَنْهُ اَلدَّوَابَّ كُلَّهَا إِلاَّ اَلْقَمْلَةَ فَإِنَّهَا مِنْ جَسَدِهِ فَإِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يُحَوِّلَ قَمْلَةً مِنْ مَكَانٍ إِلَى مَكَانٍ فَلاَ يَضُرَّهُ.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications: Hadith.2704 - Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah (as), who said: "A person in the state of ihram should remove all insects from themselves, except for lice, as they are from their body. However, if they wish to move a louse from one place to another, it does not harm them."
